prompt:
Hop currently treats the selected folder as a controller/canonical-state anchor:
hop begin creates an isolated workspace under .hop/workspaces/.
The agent edits and tests there.
hop propose freezes that workspace.
hop accept advances Hop’s internal accepted Git tree.
But it does not check that accepted tree out into the visible project root.
So the work is stored and accepted, but the folder you selected still appears empty. Codex also hides .hop, making this especially confusing.
For desktop use, I think the correct behavior is:
Agents continue working in isolated Hop workspaces.
Acceptance updates Hop’s internal state.
Hop then safely materializes the accepted tree into /Users/christopher/Dev/HopWeb.
If that visible folder contains uncommitted user changes, Hop blocks synchronization instead of overwriting them.
That gives isolation during work while ensuring the selected project folder always displays the accepted result. The current behavior is effectively “bare controller mode,” and it’s wrong for the UX you expected.
response_summary: Roll completed agent sessions onto the latest accepted state
